name: Whole or Halved Tomatoes (Packed in Water)
recipeIngredient: An average of 21 pounds for a canner load of 7 quarts
An average of 13 pounds for a canner load of 9 pints
A bushel weighs 53 pounds and yields 15 to 21 quarts, averaging 3 pounds per quart
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash tomatoes.
2. Dip in boiling water for 30 to 60 seconds or until skins split, then dip in cold water.
3. Slip off skins and remove cores. Leave whole or halve.
4. Add bottled lemon juice or citric acid to jars (see acidification directions).
5. Add 1 teaspoon of salt per quart to the jars, if desired.
6. For hot pack products, add enough water to cover tomatoes and gently boil for 5 minutes.
7. Fill jars with hot tomatoes or raw peeled tomatoes.
8. Add hot cooking liquid to hot pack or hot water for raw pack, covering tomatoes and leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
9. Adjust lids and process according to recommendations in Table 1, Table 2, or Table 3 based on the canning method used.
Table 1: Recommended processing time for Whole or Halved Tomatoes in a boiling-water canner (Hot pack).
Jar Size: Pints
- Altitude 0 - 1,000 ft: 40 minutes
- Altitude 1,001 - 3,000 ft: 45 minutes
- Altitude 3,001 - 6,000 ft: 50 minutes
- Altitude above 6,000 ft: 55 minutes
Jar Size: Quarts
- Altitude 0 - 1,000 ft: 45 minutes
- Altitude 1,001 - 3,000 ft: 50 minutes
- Altitude 3,001 - 6,000 ft: 55 minutes
- Altitude above 6,000 ft: 60 minutes
Table 2: Recommended processing time for Whole or Halved Tomatoes in a dial-gauge pressure canner (Hot pack).
Jar Size: Pints
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitude 0 - 2,000 ft: 6 lb
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitude 2,001 - 4,000 ft: 7 lb
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitude 4,001 - 6,000 ft: 8 lb
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitude 6,001 - 8,000 ft: 9 lb
Jar Size: Quarts
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitude 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitude 2,001 - 4,000 ft: 12 lb
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitude 4,001 - 6,000 ft: 13 lb
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitude 6,001 - 8,000 ft: 14 lb
Table 3: Recommended processing time for Whole or Halved Tomatoes in a weighted-gauge pressure canner (Hot pack).
Jar Size: Pints
- Process Time: 15 minutes
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitude 0 - 1,000 ft: 5 lb
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) above 1,000 ft: 10 lb
Jar Size: Quarts
- Process Time: 10 minutes
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) at Altitude 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb
- Gauge Pressure (PSI) above 1,000 ft: Not recommended.
